questionHtml:
Phosphoric acid, H3PO4, can undergo stepwise neutralization, forming amphiprotic species.

Formulate an equation for the reaction of one mole of phosphoric acid with one mole of sodium hydroxide.
markschemeHtml:
H3PO4 (aq) + NaOH (aq) → NaH2PO4 (aq) + H2O (l) ✔


Accept net ionic equation.

meta.title: Formulate an equation for the reaction of one mole of phosphoric acid with one mole of sodium hydroxide.
breadcrumbLabels: 1.1 Introduction to the particulate nature of matter and chemical change
